name: Ingest Health - Alert Notifier
description: Emails on any 'Ingest Health' detection alert (unified spike/drop/lag, and parser drift if enabled). Professional notification: affected source/device, scope, severity, what-it-means, and suggested triage steps, with a link to the alert. send_email is a core action; no connection needed.

data.name: Email SOC
data.subject: [SentinelOne] Ingest Health: {{singularity-response-trigger.data.name}} on {{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.asset.name, "a monitored source/device")}} ({{singularity-response-trigger.data.severity}})
data.body:
SentinelOne Ingest Health alert
Singularity Data Lake, automated detection
ACTION REQUIRED

An ingest-health detection has fired for {{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.asset.name, "the affected source/device")}} in the Singularity Data Lake. Details below; open the alert for the affected rows, current-vs-expected volume, and z-score.

Detection{{singularity-response-trigger.data.name}}
Severity{{singularity-response-trigger.data.severity}}
Affected source / device{{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.asset.name, "(see alert)")}}
Asset type{{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.asset.assetTypeClassifier, "")}}
Scope (site / account){{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.realTime.scope.site.name, "")}} / {{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.realTime.scope.account.name, "")}}
Detection source{{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.detectionSource.product, "STAR scheduled rule")}}
Detected at{{singularity-response-trigger.data.detectedAt}}
Alert ID{{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}
What this means

{{Function.DEFAULT(singularity-response-trigger.data.description, "An ingest-health threshold was crossed for this source/device.")}}

Suggested steps
  1. Open the alert (button below) and review the affected source/device, the current-vs-expected volume, and the z-score (alert indicators, and the Ingest Health dashboard Devices tab).
  2. Confirm whether the change is expected (maintenance, decommission, onboarding, known schedule). If expected, add the source to the ingestHealthExclusions lookup to stop alerting, or adjust ingestHealthDeviceLevel to change granularity.
  3. Volume drop or silence: check the collector/agent (SentinelOne agent, syslog forwarder, cloud connector), network egress and DNS to the SDL ingest endpoint, and any license or quota limit.
  4. Volume spike: check for misconfiguration, debug or verbose logging, a log loop, or duplicate forwarding from the source.
  5. Ingest lag: check collector batching and backpressure, the ingestion pipeline, and source clock accuracy.
  6. Document the finding and watch the next hourly run to confirm whether ingest recovered.
Open alert in console

Automated SentinelOne Hyperautomation notification (Ingest Health, Alert Notifier). Source level is the default; device level applies to sources in ingestHealthDeviceLevel. Please do not reply.
